inputs.label: VIP senders
inputs.description: People whose emails always get flagged (boss, investors, key clients), one per line
inputs.placeholder:
Sarah Chen (CEO)
Mike Ross (Lead Investor)
Alex Kim (Key Client)

inputs.label: Triage categories
inputs.description: Categories for sorting incoming email, one per line
inputs.default:
Action Required
FYI Only
Scheduling
Sales/Marketing
Newsletters

inputs.label: Reply style
inputs.description: How long your replies typically are

inputs.label: Quiet hours
inputs.description: Don't send notifications during these hours (24h format)

morning-triage.description: Read unread emails, categorize, flag VIPs, draft replies for straightforward ones, and present a summary

activities.intent: Read all unread emails from the inbox via Google Workspace
steps: Connect to the user's email via Google Workspace skill
Fetch all unread emails since last triage
Extract sender, subject, body preview, timestamp, and any attachments

activities.intent: Categorize each email and flag VIP senders
steps: Categorize each email into the user's configured categories (Action Required, FYI, Scheduling, Sales/Marketing, Newsletters)
Flag any emails from VIP senders regardless of category
Identify emails with urgent keywords (ASAP, urgent, deadline, overdue)
For straightforward emails (scheduling, simple questions), draft replies in the user's voice and reply_style

activities.intent: Compile and present the morning inbox summary
steps: Build the summary: total new emails, how many need action, how many have draft replies ready, how many are FYI
List VIP emails at the top with one-line previews
Group remaining emails by category
Present draft replies for approval
Store triage results in memory for the draft-replies workflow

urgent-watch.description: Check for new emails from VIP senders or with urgent keywords — only alert if genuinely urgent

activities.intent: Quick scan for urgent or VIP emails since last check
steps: Check for new emails since last scan
Filter for VIP senders from the user's list
Filter for urgent keywords (ASAP, urgent, deadline, overdue, time-sensitive)
Only alert the user if something is genuinely urgent — don't cry wolf
Respect quiet_hours setting — suppress non-critical notifications

draft-replies.description: Draft replies for action-required emails in the user's voice, matching sender style

activities.intent: Draft replies for each action-required email in the user's communication style
steps: For each email categorized as 'Action Required', analyze the sender's tone and length
Draft a reply matching the user's reply_style preference (brief, detailed, or match-sender)
Use the user's name and role_title appropriately in sign-offs
Run each draft through copy-editing for grammar and tone consistency
Present all drafts for user approval — never auto-send

weekly-inbox-report.description: Summarize last week's inbox: volume, response times, categories, unanswered emails, and unsubscribe suggestions

activities.intent: Gather and analyze one week of inbox data for the report
steps: Pull email data from the past 7 days
Calculate: total received, total sent, average response time
Break down by category (Action Required, FYI, Scheduling, Sales/Marketing, Newsletters)
Flag any emails unanswered for more than 48 hours
Identify repeat senders who could be unsubscribed

activities.intent: Compile findings into a scannable weekly inbox report
steps: Create the report with these sections:
1. HEADLINE: One sentence summary of the week (e.g., 'Inbox volume down 15% — 2 emails still need replies')
2. BY THE NUMBERS: Total received, sent, response time, category breakdown
3. NEEDS ATTENTION: Emails unanswered >48h with context
4. CLEANUP SUGGESTIONS: Newsletters to unsubscribe, repeat low-value senders
5. TRENDS: Compare to previous weeks if data available in memory
Store report in memory for trend tracking
Present to user

cleanup.description: Identify stale newsletters, archivable threads, and suggest better inbox organization

activities.intent: Scan for newsletters not opened in 3+ months, archivable threads, and organization improvements
steps: Identify newsletter subscriptions the user hasn't opened in 3+ months
Find email threads that can be safely archived (resolved, old, no pending action)
Suggest labels or folders for better organization based on email patterns
Present all suggestions for approval — never auto-archive or unsubscribe
